steps.title: Narrow the general claim
steps.content: The first sentence says, broadly, that auroras result from the Sun's activity. The next sentence explains the exact mechanism: charged particles from the Sun are captured by Earth's magnetic field and channeled toward the poles.

steps.title: Reject wrong signals
steps.content:
• Choice B signals similarity, but the second sentence gives a more precise explanation.
  • Choice C signals contrast, but no contradiction is being introduced.
  • Choice D signals a conclusion, but the sentence explains a cause.
The remaining choice introduces the specific mechanism. The answer is A.
